q:
speak
 Tanya ___ German very well.
a: Tanya speaks German very well.

q:
go
 Ben and Jack ___ to the same school.
a: Ben and Jack go to the same school.

q:
cause
 Bad driving ___ many accidents.
a: Bad driving causes many accidents.

q:
close
 The museum ___ at 4 o'clock on Sundays.
a: The museum closes at 4 o'clock on Sundays.

q:
live
 My parents ___ in a very small flat.
a: My parents live in a very small flat.

q:
take
 The Olympic Games ___ place every four years.
a: The Olympic Games take place every four years.

q:
connect
 The Panama Canal ___ the Atlantic and Pacific oceans.
a: The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic and Pacific oceans.

q:
not / drink
 Julia ___ tea very often.
a: Julia doesn't drink tea very often.

q:
the banks / close
 What time ___ here?
a: What time do the banks close here?

q:
not / use
 I have a car, but I ___ it much.
a: I have a car, but I don't use it much.

q:
Maria / come
 Where ___ from? Is she Spanish?
a: Where does Maria come from? Is she Spanish?

q:
you / do
 'What ___?' 'I'm an electrician.'
a: 'What do you do?' 'I'm an electrician.'

q:
this word / mean
 Look at this sentence. What ___?
a: Look at this sentence. What does this word mean?

q:
not / do
 David isn't very fit. He ___ any sport.
a: David isn't very fit. He doesn't do any sport.

q:
it / take
 It takes me an hour to get to work in the morning. How long ___ you?
a: It takes me an hour to get to work in the morning. How long does it take you?

q:
go
 The earth ___ round the sun.
a: The earth goes round the sun.

q:
not / grow
 Rice ___ in cold climates.
a: Rice doesn't grow in cold climates.

q:
rise
 The sun ___ in the east.
a: The sun rises in the east.

q:
make
 Bees ___ honey.
a: Bees make honey.

q:
not / eat
 Vegetarians ___ meat.
a: Vegetarians don't eat meat.

q:
not / believe
 An atheist ___ in God.
a: An atheist doesn't believe in God.

q:
translate
 An interpreter ___ from one language into another.
a: An interpreter translates from one language into another.

q:
not / tell
 Liars are people who ___ the truth.
a: Liars are people who don't tell the truth.

q:
flow
 The River Amazon ___ into the Atlantic Ocean.
a: The River Amazon flows into the Atlantic Ocean.

q:
you / play / tennis
 How often ___?
a: How often do you play tennis?

q:
your sister / play / tennis
 Perhaps Lisa's sister plays tennis too. You want to know. ___?
a: Does your sister play tennis?

q:
you / go / to the cinema
 You know that Lisa goes to the cinema a lot. You want to know how often. ___?
a: How often do you go to the cinema?

q:
your brother / do
 You know that Lisa's brother works. You want to know what he does. ___?
a: What does your brother do?

q:
you / speak / Spanish
 You're not sure whether Lisa speaks Spanish. You want to know. ___?
a: Do you speak Spanish?

q:
where / your grandparents / live
 You don't know where Lisa's grandparents live. You want to know. ___?
a: Where do your grandparents live?

q:
suggest
 Mr Evans is not in the office today. I ___ you try calling him tomorrow.
a: Mr Evans is not in the office today. I suggest you try calling him tomorrow.

q:
promise
 I ___ I won't tell anybody what you said.
a: I promise I won't tell anybody what you said.

q:
insist
 (in a restaurant) I ___ you must let me pay for the meal.
a: (in a restaurant) I insist you must let me pay for the meal.

q:
apologise
 I ___ for what I said. I shouldn't have said it.
a: I apologise for what I said. I shouldn't have said it.

q:
agree
 I ___ with you. I think you're absolutely right.
a: I agree with you. I think you're absolutely right.
